input.background: Data from a historically important study by Sir Richard Doll published in 1955 are used to illustrate techniques in this chapter. This study looked at lung cancer mortality rates by region (response variable) according to per capita cigarette consumption (explanatory variable). Table 14.2 lists the data.
input.question: Inspect the relationship between cigarette consumption $(X)$ and lung cancer mortality $(Y)$ with inferential techniques.

output.method: 回归系数显著性与方向解读
output.answer: Inferential techniques may involve regression analysis or hypothesis testing to infer the nature of the relationship.

COUNTRY.description: The name of the country where data was collected.

CIG1930.description: Per capita cigarette consumption in 1930 for each country.

LUNGCA.description: Lung cancer cases per 100,000 in 1950 for each country.

meta_info.caption: Data (Doll’s ecological study of smoking and lung cancer).
